outputs.description: Alternative ID of the actor. Okta.Logs.Events.actor.displayName Display name of the actor.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.actor.alternateId

outputs.description: ID of the actor. Okta.Logs.Events.client.userAgent.rawUserAgent A raw string representation of user agent, formatted according to section 5.5.3 of HTTP/1.1 Semantics and Content. Both the browser and the OS fields can be derived from this field.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.actor.id

outputs.description: The operating system on which the client runs. For example, Microsoft Windows 10. Okta.Logs.Events.client.userAgent.browser Identifies the type of web browser, if relevant. For example, Chrome.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.client.userAgent.os

outputs.description: Type of device from which the client operated. For example, Computer. Okta.Logs.Events.client.id For OAuth requests, the ID of the OAuth client making the request. For SSWS token requests, the ID of the agent making the request.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.client.device

outputs.description: IP address from which the client made its request. Okta.Logs.Events.client.geographicalContext.city The city encompassing the area containing the geo-location coordinates, if available. For example, Seattle, San Francisco.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.client.ipAddress

outputs.description: Full name of the state or province encompassing the area containing the geo-location coordinates. For example, Montana, Incheon. Okta.Logs.Events.client.geographicalContext.country Full name of the country encompassing the area containing the geo-location coordinates. For example, France, Uganda.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.client.geographicalContext.state

outputs.description: The display message for an event. Okta.Logs.Events.eventType Type of event that was published.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.displayMessage

outputs.description: Result of the action. Can be "SUCCESS", "FAILURE", "SKIPPED", or "UNKNOWN". Okta.Logs.Events.outcome.reason Reason for the result. For example, INVALID_CREDENTIALS.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.outcome.result

outputs.description: Timestamp when the event was published. Okta.Logs.Events.severity The event severity. Can be "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARN", or "ERROR".
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.published

outputs.description: Autonomous system number associated with the autonomous system that the event request was sourced to. Okta.Logs.Events.securityContext.asOrg Organization associated with the autonomous system that the event request was sourced to.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.securityContext.asNumber

outputs.description: Internet service provider used to send the event's request. Okta.Logs.Events.securityContext.domain Specifies whether an event's request is from a known proxy.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.securityContext.isp

outputs.description: IP address. Okta.Logs.Events.request.ipChain.geographicalContext.city The city encompassing the area containing the geo-location coordinates, if available. For example, Seattle, San Francisco.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.request.ipChain.IP

outputs.description: Full name of the state or province encompassing the area containing the geo-location coordinates. For example, Montana, Incheon. Okta.Logs.Events.request.ipChain.geographicalContext.country Full name of the country encompassing the area containing the geo-location coordinates. For example, France, Uganda.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.request.ipChain.geographicalContext.state

outputs.description: Details regarding the source. Okta.Logs.Events.target.id ID of a target.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.request.ipChain.source

outputs.description: Type of a target. Okta.Logs.Events.target.alternateId Alternative ID of a target.
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.target.type

outputs.description: Display name of a target. Edit this page Report an Issue
outputs.name: Okta.Logs.Events.target.displayName
